methodsAttempted.query: site:unc.edu University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill generative AI academic integrity policy students ChatGPT
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official ai.unc.edu research, faculty, and staff guidance pages, a School of Nursing page, a University Policy Office news/advisory item, and library/writing-center resources.

Firecrawl search found official ai.unc.edu research and staff guidance, an official faculty grading and assessment PDF, an ITS Copilot news item, and school/unit pages that were not used for university-level claims.
Firecrawl search confirmed the strongest official-source evidence was on ai.unc.edu guidance pages and the official ai.unc.edu assessment PDF.
The official Generative AI at UNC domain was checked directly. robots.txt permits the crawled public pages and uploads path for generic crawlers; no login wall or access-control bypass was attempted.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found four policy-specific UNC-Chapel Hill sources on ai.unc.edu: research GenAI usage guidance, faculty course AI guidance, staff administrative GenAI guidance, and a faculty grading and assessment PDF. Approved claims are scoped to those audiences and uses. Unit, news/advisory, library, writing-center, and ITS news pages were documented but not used as approved claim evidence.

stopConditions: Stop after official ai.unc.edu policy-specific sources have been captured.
Do not use unit-level, news, library, writing-center, or ITS announcement pages as approved policy evidence when central guidance is available.
Do not bypass login walls, access controls, robots restrictions, or blocked sources.

artifacts.claimText: UNC-Chapel Hill's research generative AI guidance applies to members of the research community involved in research under the auspices of the University, including faculty, staff, students, guest researchers, collaborators, and consultants.

citation.citationTitle: Research Generative AI Usage Guidance - Generative AI at UNC
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UNC-Chapel Hill's research guidance treats entering private or confidential information, research data, grant proposals, or analytical results into public generative AI tools as a public disclosure of that information.

citation.citationTitle: Research Generative AI Usage Guidance - Generative AI at UNC
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UNC-Chapel Hill faculty guidance encourages instructors to state course and assignment AI expectations in the syllabus and tells Carolina students to follow the specific AI guidelines in that syllabus.

citation.citationTitle: Developing AI Guidance for Faculty - Generative AI at UNC

artifacts.claimText: UNC-Chapel Hill's faculty grading and assessment guidance says AI systems used for grading or feedback must be institutionally approved and compliant with data security and privacy standards; faculty using GenAI for grading retain full responsibility for evaluative decisions and feedback.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Research Generative AI Usage Guidance - Generative AI at UNC
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: This guidance applies to all members of the research community, including faculty, staff (SHRA and EHRA non-faculty), students (undergraduate, graduate and professional), guest researchers (e.g., unpaid volunteers, interns, and visiting scholars), collaborators, and consultants involved in research occurring under the auspices of the University.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Research Generative AI Usage Guidance, opening guidance scope

artifacts.sourceTitle: Research Generative AI Usage Guidance - Generative AI at UNC
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Uploading information (e.g., research data, grant proposals, unpublished manuscripts, or analytical results) to a public AI tool is equivalent to releasing it publicly; thus, before any information from you or another individual is uploaded to a public AI tool, appropriate steps must be taken to ensure that the disclosure of that information is consistent with all rules and laws related to the handling of private information.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Research Use Guidance, limitations and risks

artifacts.sourceTitle: Developing AI Guidance for Faculty - Generative AI at UNC
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Conveying your stance on students’ use of AI in your course is important; it clarifies your expectations and ensures that any use of AI supports rather than frustrates your learning objectives.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The same page's syllabus starter language tells students to follow specific AI guidelines in the syllabus and check with the instructor if unsure.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Developing AI Guidance for Faculty, introduction and syllabus starter language

artifacts.sourceTitle: Staff Generative AI Usage Guidance - Generative AI at UNC
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Do not enter sensitive information (as defined by the UNC-Chapel Hill Information Classification Standard) into generative AI tools unless the University’s Information Security Office (ISO) has conducted a risk assessment of the generative AI tool and the University’s Data Governance Oversight Group (DGOG) has approved the tool to handle sensitive information.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Staff Generative AI Usage Guidance, Protecting Confidential, Copyrighted, or Personal Data

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance for Faculty Use of AI in Grading and Assessment
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Faculty must ensure that any AI system used for grading or feedback is institutionally approved and compliant with data security and privacy standards. Uploading student work to consumer-grade AI platforms not contracted by the university is inconsistent with student privacy laws and university policy.

artifacts.notes: Approved as audience-scoped research guidance directly supported by official ai.unc.edu text.
Approved because the source explicitly equates uploading private or research information to a public AI tool with public release.
Approved as teaching guidance; wording uses encourages/expected rather than overstating a standalone university-wide student rule.
Approved as administrative-work security review guidance with direct ISO/DGOG approval language.
Approved as faculty assessment guidance supported by official PDF language on approved systems, privacy, and faculty responsibility.
Approved as narrowly scoped faculty assessment guidance on instructor-led review after AI-generated grading or feedback concerns.

artifacts.title: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill Generative AI Guidance Staged Findings
artifacts.summary: Staged findings identify official UNC-Chapel Hill generative AI guidance for research, faculty course guidance, staff administrative work, and faculty grading and assessment. Claims remain source-scoped and are not published as canonical public claims.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are scoped to the official sources captured in this staged run and should not be treated as canonical until reviewed and promoted through the public release process.
School-level, news/advisory, library, writing-center, and ITS announcement sources were rejected for approved claims where central ai.unc.edu guidance was available.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
